mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing price-to-earnings multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly CPI inflation above 0.5% forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, depressing long-duration equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.1) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and a decline in nonfarm payroll employment indicates labor market deterioration, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when UNRATE increases by >0.2pp while PAYEMS grows, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals supply chain normalization and easing input cost pressures, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in >50% of observations, or the signal reverses direction during monetary tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth and falling housing starts reflect weakening aggregate demand, reducing corporate revenue forecasts and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of periods with declining GDPC1 and HOUST, or rolling IC drops below 0.01 over 24 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 3.5% amid persistent CPI levels above 325, real rates turn restrictive, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% while CPIAUCSL remains above 325
failure_condition: The threshold condition fails to predict negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL falls below 300.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.0% indicates a tight labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in >50% of periods where UNRATE stays below 4.0%, or the signal breaks down when inflation exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal spending and housing starts signal robust consumer demand and construction activity, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.
